input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 10 (which targets Android API 35+), edge-to-edge display is enforced by default, and the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach was deprecated. The new approach involves using `WindowInsets` and handling keyboard (IME) insets properly.


The deprecation happened because Android API 30 removed support for the old soft input mode adjustment, and by API 35 it's completely ignored. The solution now requires configuring the manifest with the appropriate soft input mode and then properly handling insets in the application code. Wrapping content in a `ScrollView` is one option, but .NET MAUI's `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` handles keyboard scrolling automatically since .NET 8. For a chat interface where the layout needs to resize rather than scroll, I should use `WindowInsetsCompat.Ime` to detect keyboard height and adjust padding dynamically — that's the approach for .NET 10.
